twitter_thread: £19,343 in passive income annually: achievable with 40% in high-yield bonds, 30% in dividend-paying stocks, and a 10-year timeline.
Vanguard's FTSE UK Eq Inc fund yields 4.8%, while iWeb's platform charges 0.1% per trade. Worth considering.
Savers who put £10,000 in a 1% savings account will earn £1,000 less than those investing in a 5% bond over 5 years.
JP Morgan's 8%+ yield ETFs have a 1.2% management fee. Is the upside worth the cost?
Split investments into 5-7 assets: 20% UK equities, 20% US equities, 20% bonds, 10% property, 10% alternatives, 20% cash.
ISA tax savings: £1,000 per year for a £50,000 income. That's 2% of your income.
What's your highest-earning passive income stream? #passiveincome #investing
